message:
fix(tui): use full results-strip width for pane scroll capacity

visible_pane_capacity() was using the active pane's inner rect width
(a single pane) instead of the full results-strip width, causing it
to compute capacity=1 in multi-pane mode. When the detail pane was
open, this mismatch between navigation-time capacity (too small) and
render-time capacity (correct) meant the scroll offset wouldn't
advance far enough to reveal off-screen panes during navigation.

Fix: store the full results-strip inner rect before the pane loop
instead of storing individual pane inner rects per-pane. Also add
adjust_pane_scroll_offset() after directional focus navigation.

Fixes #107

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
